metadata.title: 天にはさかえ
metadata.subtitle: Hark! The Herald Angels Sing — Japanese (聖公会讃美歌, 1891 「かみにはさかえ」)
metadata.lyricist: Charles Wesley (1707–1788) — Japanese translation by the editor of 聖公会讃美歌 (Hugh J. Foss, 1891), at the time titled かみにはさかえ
metadata.x_pd_basis: Original lyrics by Wesley (d. 1788) and tune by Mendelssohn (d. 1847) are PD by > 150 years. Earliest Japanese translation appeared in ヒウ・ゼ・フヲス編『聖公会讃美歌』(1891) no. 35 — > 130 years ago, Meiji 24. Japanese Wikisource hosts the full PD text
metadata.x_translator: Hugh J. Foss (ヒウ・ゼ・フヲス) ed., 聖公会讃美歌 (1891)

chordpro:
{title: 天にはさかえ}
{subtitle: Hark! The Herald Angels Sing — Japanese, 聖公会讃美歌 (1891) no. 35 「かみにはさかえ」}
{key: F}
{time: 4/4}
{tempo: 100}
{comment: Title in the original Meiji-era hymnal: 「かみにはさかえ」. Modernized to 「天にはさかえ」 in later editions.}

{start_of_verse: 1}
[F]かみには[C]さかえ [Dm]ちには[Bb]おだやか
kami ni wa sakae, chi ni wa odayaka
[F]よひと〻[C]かみと [Dm]やはらぎ[Bb]たりと
yo hitobito kami to, yawaragi tari to
[F]みつかひ[Bb]たちの [F]うたへる[C]うたを
mitsukai tachi no, utaeru uta wo
[Dm]ひと〴〵[Am]き〻て [Bb]ともによ[C]ろこ[F]び
hitobito kikite, tomo ni yorokobi
[F]けふあれ[Bb]ませる [F]きみをほ[C]むべ[F]し
kyou are maseru, kimi wo homu beshi
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 2}
[F]さだめた[C]まひし [Dm]そのとき[Bb]にしも
sadame tamaishi, sono toki ni shimo
[F]たふとき[C]かみの [Dm]みくらを[Bb]はなれ
tautoki kami no, mikura wo hanare
[F]われらと[Bb]〻もに [F]ましま[C]すために
warera tomo ni, mashimasu tame ni
[Dm]しづのを[Am]とめの [Bb]はらにや[C]どり[F]て
shizu no otome no, hara ni yadorite
[F]けふあれ[Bb]ませる [F]きみをほ[C]むべ[F]し
kyou are maseru, kimi wo homu beshi
{end_of_verse}

{start_of_verse: 3}
[F]くもきり[C]はらい [Dm]ひの〻[Bb]ぼるごと
kumo kiri harai, hi no noboru goto
[F]みひかり[C]をもて [Dm]くらきを[Bb]てらし
mihikari wo mote, kuraki wo terashi
[F]つちより[Bb]いづる [F]ひとをい[C]かしめ
tsuchi yori izuru, hito wo ikashime
[Dm]つきぬい[Am]のちを [Bb]さづくる[C]ため[F]に
tsukinu inochi wo, sazukuru tame ni
[F]けふあれ[Bb]ませる [F]きみをほ[C]むべ[F]し
kyou are maseru, kimi wo homu beshi
{end_of_verse}
